architecture.summary: MVF is a five-layer architectural standard that transforms probabilistic LLM outputs into deterministic software artifacts. Information flows through: Kernel Identity (system state anchoring), Synaptic Routing (probabilistic→deterministic intent mapping), Interface Contracts (output schema enforcement), Context Anchoring (audit trail binding), and Mirror Test (structural validation). The system aims to make LLM outputs observable and auditable for enterprise use.

claims.statement: Large Language Models suffer from inherent stochasticity, limiting their utility in high-stakes enterprise environments where determinism and auditability are required
The MFOUR Vibe Framework is a platform-agnostic architectural standard that transforms probabilistic natural language intent into deterministic software artifacts
MVF defines a five-layer topology comprising the Kernel Identity, Synaptic Routing, Interface Contracts, Context Anchoring, and the Mirror Test
The Vibe Integrity Score (VIS) is a quantitative metric for evaluating the structural adherence of generative outputs
The specification provides foundational schema and logic protocols for building Glass Box AI systems that are observable, secure, and commercially viable
